items.title: Code of Conduct
items.text: In the interest of fostering an open and welcoming environment, we as contributors and maintainers pledge to making participation in our project and our community a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body size, disability, ethnicity, gender identity and expression, level of experience, education, socioeconomic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, or sexual identity and orientation.

items.title: Our Standards
items.text:
Examples of behavior that contributes to creating a positive environment include:

  • using welcoming and inclusive language,
  • being respectful of differing viewpoints and experiences,
  • gracefully accepting constructive criticism,
  • focusing on what is best for the community, and
  • showing empathy towards other community members.

Examples of unacceptable behavior by participants include:

  • the use of sexualized language or imagery and unwelcome sexual attention or advances,
  • trolling, insulting/derogatory comments, and personal or political attacks,
  • public or private harassment,
  • publishing others' private information, such as a physical or electronic address, without explicit permission, and
  • other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional setting

items.title: Our Responsibilities
items.text:
Project maintainers are responsible for clarifying the standards of acceptable behavior and are expected to take appropriate and fair corrective action in response to any instances of unacceptable behavior.

Project maintainers have the right and responsibility to remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, and other contributions that are not aligned to this Code of Conduct, or to ban temporarily or permanently any contributor for other behaviors that they deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, or harmful.

items.title: Scope
items.text: This Code of Conduct applies both within project spaces and in public spaces when an individual is representing the project or its community. Examples of representing a project or community include using an official project email address, posting via an official social media account, or acting as an appointed representative at an online or offline event. Representation of a project may be further defined and clarified by project maintainers.

items.title: Enforcement
items.text:
Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be reported by emailing the project team. All complaints will be reviewed and investigated and will result in a response that is deemed necessary and appropriate to the circumstances. The project team is obligated to maintain confidentiality with regard to the reporter of an incident. Further details of specific enforcement policies may be posted separately.

Project maintainers who do not follow or enforce the Code of Conduct in good faith may face temporary or permanent repercussions as determined by other members of the project's leadership.

items.title: Attribution
items.text: This Code of Conduct is adapted from the Contributor Covenant version 1.4.

items.title: All Of
items.text: Wait for all of a set of events to complete.

items.title: AllOf
items.text:
Bases: Event

An Event that triggers when all named child Events have triggered.

Child events are passed as keyword arguments. The value of the AllOf event is a dict mapping each keyword to the corresponding result.

items.title: cancelled property
items.text: True if the event has been cancelled.

items.title: triggered property
items.text: True if the event has been triggered (not pending, not cancelled).

items.title: cancel()
items.text:
Cancel the event.

Fires _on_cancel(old_value) regardless of whether the event was pending or already triggered. This ensures that resources consumed by a pre-triggered get event are restored when FirstOf discards it. Does nothing if the event is already cancelled.

items.title: fail(exc)
items.text:
Trigger the event with an exception.

The process awaiting this event will re-raise exc.

items.title: succeed(value=None)
items.text: Trigger the event with value and notify all waiters.

items.title: Barrier
items.text: Barrier that holds multiple processes until released.

items.title: Barrier
items.text:
A barrier that blocks any number of processes until release() is called.

Processes call await barrier.wait() to park. When release() is called, all currently waiting processes are unblocked simultaneously.

items.title: release()
items.text: Trigger all currently waiting events.

items.title: wait()
items.text: Return an Event that resolves when release() is called.

items.title: Container
items.text: Homogeneous resource (continuous or discrete amounts).

items.title: Container
items.text:
A resource holding up to capacity units of homogeneous content.

Works for both continuous amounts (float) and discrete counts (int). Blocking operations return an Event whose value is the amount transferred. Non-blocking operations raise on failure.

Cancelled get events restore the level via _on_cancel so that FirstOf does not silently discard consumed content.

items.title: capacity property
items.text: Maximum capacity.

items.title: level property
items.text: Current content level.

items.title: get(amount)
items.text:
Return an Event that resolves to amount when content is available.

If sufficient content is available immediately, the Event is pre-triggered and _on_cancel is set to restore the level if FirstOf later discards the result.

items.title: put(amount)
items.text: Return an Event that resolves to amount when space is available.

items.title: try_get(amount)
items.text: Remove and return amount of content, or raise ContainerEmpty.

items.title: try_put(amount)
items.text: Add amount of content, or raise ContainerFull.

items.title: ContainerEmpty
items.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised by Container.try_get() when there is insufficient content.

items.title: ContainerFull
items.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised by Container.try_put() when there is insufficient space.

items.title: Core
items.text: Core simulation primitives: Environment, Event, Interrupt, Timeout, Process.

items.title: Environment
items.text:
Discrete-event simulation environment.

Maintains two queues: - _ready: callbacks to run at the current simulated time (deque). - _heap: callbacks scheduled for a future time (min-heap).

The clock only advances when popping from _heap; _ready is always drained first. This prevents zero-delay events from racing ahead of same-time future events and ensures FIFO ordering among simultaneous events.

items.title: now property
items.text: Current simulation time.

items.title: immediate(cb)
items.text: Schedule cb for execution at the current simulated time.

items.title: run(until=None)
items.text:
Run the simulation.

Runs until no events remain, or until simulated time reaches until.

items.title: schedule(time, cb)
items.text: Schedule cb to run at time in the future.

items.title: timeout(delay)
items.text: Return a Timeout event for delay time units.

items.title: Event
items.text:
An awaitable simulation event.

Primitives such as Queue.get() return Event objects. Processes suspend themselves by awaiting an Event:

item = await queue.get()

An Event transitions through these states (stored in _value): _PENDING: not yet triggered any value: triggered with that value (including None) _CANCELLED: cancelled; _on_cancel was called if set

The _on_cancel callback is called by cancel() even when the event has already been triggered. This lets resource-consuming get() methods restore their resource when FirstOf discards a non-winning event.

items.title: cancelled property
items.text: True if the event has been cancelled.

items.title: triggered property
items.text: True if the event has been triggered (not pending, not cancelled).

items.title: cancel()
items.text:
Cancel the event.

Fires _on_cancel(old_value) regardless of whether the event was pending or already triggered. This ensures that resources consumed by a pre-triggered get event are restored when FirstOf discards it. Does nothing if the event is already cancelled.

items.title: fail(exc)
items.text:
Trigger the event with an exception.

The process awaiting this event will re-raise exc.

items.title: succeed(value=None)
items.text: Trigger the event with value and notify all waiters.

items.title: Interrupt
items.text:
Bases: Exception

Exception thrown into a process by Process.interrupt().

items.title: Process
items.text:
Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for all simulation processes.

Subclasses implement run() as an async method. Optionally override init() for setup that must happen before the coroutine is created.

Example::

class Worker(Process):
    def init(self, name):
        self.name = name

    async def run(self):
        while True:
            await self.timeout(1.0)
            print(f"{self.name} at t={self.now}")

env = Environment()
Worker(env, "alice")
env.run(until=10)

items.title: now property
items.text: Shortcut to the current simulation time.

items.title: init(*args, **kwargs)
items.text: Optional subclass setup hook, called before the coroutine is created.

items.title: interrupt(cause=None)
items.text:
Throw an Interrupt into this process.

The Interrupt is delivered at the process's current await point. Has no effect if the process has already finished.

items.title: resume(value=None)
items.text:
Called by an Event when it triggers; re-schedules _loop.

Uses functools.partial (a C-level callable) to avoid creating a Python closure, keeping per-resume overhead low.

items.title: run() abstractmethod async
items.text: Implement process behaviour here.

items.title: timeout(delay)
items.text: Return a Timeout event for delay simulated time units.

items.title: Timeout
items.text:
Bases: Event

An Event that triggers after delay simulated time units.

items.title: cancelled property
items.text: True if the event has been cancelled.

items.title: triggered property
items.text: True if the event has been triggered (not pending, not cancelled).

items.title: cancel()
items.text:
Cancel the event.

Fires _on_cancel(old_value) regardless of whether the event was pending or already triggered. This ensures that resources consumed by a pre-triggered get event are restored when FirstOf discards it. Does nothing if the event is already cancelled.

items.title: fail(exc)
items.text:
Trigger the event with an exception.

The process awaiting this event will re-raise exc.

items.title: succeed(value=None)
items.text: Trigger the event with value and notify all waiters.

items.title: First Of
items.text: Wait for the first of a set of events to complete.

items.title: FirstOf
items.text:
Bases: Event

An Event that triggers when the first named child Event triggers.

The value of the FirstOf event is a (key, value) tuple identifying which child won and what its value was. All other child events are cancelled.

items.title: cancelled property
items.text: True if the event has been cancelled.

items.title: triggered property
items.text: True if the event has been triggered (not pending, not cancelled).

items.title: cancel()
items.text:
Cancel the event.

Fires _on_cancel(old_value) regardless of whether the event was pending or already triggered. This ensures that resources consumed by a pre-triggered get event are restored when FirstOf discards it. Does nothing if the event is already cancelled.

items.title: fail(exc)
items.text:
Trigger the event with an exception.

The process awaiting this event will re-raise exc.

items.title: succeed(value=None)
items.text: Trigger the event with value and notify all waiters.

items.title: Preemptive
items.text: Preemptive shared resource.

items.title: Preempted dataclass
items.text:
Interrupt cause delivered when a process is evicted from a PreemptiveResource.

Attributes:

Name Type Description by Process

the process that caused the preemption.

usage_since float

simulation time when the preempted process acquired the resource.

items.title: PreemptiveResource
items.text:
Shared resource where higher-priority processes can preempt lower-priority users.

Priority is an integer; lower values are served first (0 is highest priority). When a new acquire request has better priority than the worst current user and preempt=True, that user is interrupted with a Preempted cause and removed from the resource. The preempted process is responsible for catching the Interrupt, tracking remaining work, and re-acquiring.

Do not call release() when handling a Preempted interrupt: the preempted process has already been removed from the user list by the preemptor.

items.title: count property
items.text: Current number of active users.

items.title: acquire(priority=0, preempt=True) async
items.text:
Acquire one unit of the resource.

Must be called from within a Process.run() coroutine; the calling process is identified via env.active_process.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default priority int

lower value = higher priority (0 is best).

0 preempt bool

if True, may interrupt the lowest-priority current user when the resource is full and that user has lower priority than this request.

True

items.title: release()
items.text:
Release one unit of the resource.

The calling process is identified via env.active_process. Do not call this when handling a Preempted interrupt: the preempted process has already been removed from the user list by the preemptor.

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

if the calling process is not a current user.

items.title: Queue
items.text: FIFO queues with blocking and non-blocking operations.

items.title: PriorityQueue
items.text:
Bases: Queue

Queue that serves items in sorted (ascending) order.

Uses bisect.insort to maintain the internal list in sorted order. Items must be comparable.

items.title: get()
items.text: Return an Event whose value is the next dequeued item.

items.title: is_empty()
items.text: True if the queue has no items.

items.title: is_full()
items.text: True if the queue is at capacity.

items.title: put(item)
items.text:
Return an Event that resolves to True when item is enqueued.

Delivers directly to a waiting getter if one exists, adds to the queue if there is capacity, or blocks until capacity becomes available.

items.title: size()
items.text: Number of items currently in the queue.

items.title: try_get()
items.text: Remove and return the next item, or raise QueueEmpty.

items.title: try_put(item)
items.text: Add item to the queue, or raise QueueFull.

items.title: Queue
items.text:
FIFO queue with optional maximum capacity.

Blocking operations (get, put) return an Event; await it for the result. Non-blocking operations (try_get, try_put) raise on failure.

items.title: get()
items.text: Return an Event whose value is the next dequeued item.

items.title: is_empty()
items.text: True if the queue has no items.

items.title: is_full()
items.text: True if the queue is at capacity.

items.title: put(item)
items.text:
Return an Event that resolves to True when item is enqueued.

Delivers directly to a waiting getter if one exists, adds to the queue if there is capacity, or blocks until capacity becomes available.

items.title: size()
items.text: Number of items currently in the queue.

items.title: try_get()
items.text: Remove and return the next item, or raise QueueEmpty.

items.title: try_put(item)
items.text: Add item to the queue, or raise QueueFull.

items.title: QueueEmpty
items.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised by Queue.try_get() when the queue is empty.

items.title: QueueFull
items.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised by Queue.try_put() when the queue is at capacity.

items.title: Resource
items.text: Shared resource with limited capacity (discrete slots).

items.title: Resource
items.text:
A shared resource with capacity concurrent-use slots.

Processes acquire a slot (blocking if all slots are taken) and release it when done. Supports async context manager protocol.

items.title: count property
items.text: Number of slots currently in use.

items.title: acquire()
items.text: Return an Event that resolves to None when a slot is available.

items.title: release()
items.text: Release one slot and wake the next waiting process (lazy deletion).

items.title: try_acquire()
items.text: Acquire a slot if one is free. Returns True on success, False otherwise.

items.title: Store
items.text: Store of heterogeneous objects with optional filter on get.

items.title: Store
items.text:
A collection of heterogeneous objects.

Blocking get() accepts an optional filter callable. The first item for which filter(item) is True (or any item when filter is None) is removed and returned. If no matching item is available, the process blocks.

Blocking put() blocks only if the store is at capacity.

items.title: get(filter=None)
items.text:
Return an Event whose value is the first matching item.

If a matching item is already available, the Event is pre-triggered and _on_cancel is set so that FirstOf can restore the item.

items.title: put(item)
items.text:
Return an Event that resolves to True when item is stored.

Delivers directly to a matching waiting getter if one exists, adds to items if there is capacity, or blocks.

items.title: try_get(filter=None)
items.text: Remove and return the first matching item, or raise StoreEmpty.

items.title: try_put(item)
items.text: Add item to the store, or raise StoreFull.

items.title: StoreEmpty
items.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised by Store.try_get() when no matching item is available.

items.title: StoreFull
items.text:
Bases: Exception

Raised by Store.try_put() when the store is at capacity.
